meta.description: Preemptive cleanup, due to previous errors – Most likely to see when running multiple cuda applications and hitting a DBE
meta.investigatory_action: Solo: RESTART_FM Not Solo: IGNORE (follow other Xid)
meta.trigger_conditions: This event is logged when the user application aborts and the kernel driver tears down the GPU application running on the GPU. Control-C, GPU resets, sigkill are all examples where the application is aborted and this event is created. In many cases, this is not indicative of a bug but rather a user or system action.

Oct 10 12:34:56 hostname kernel: [123456.789012] NVRM: Xid (PCI:0000:00:1F.0): 48, 0x00000001, 0x00000001, 0x00000001, 0x00000001
Oct 10 12:34:56 hostname kernel: [123456.789013] NVRM: Uncorrectable ECC Error detected on GPU.
Oct 10 12:34:56 hostname kernel: [123456.789014] NVRM: Workaround required.
Uncorrectable ECC Error logged (Xid: 48). Please perform a GPU reset or a node reboot.

meta.trigger_conditions: This event is logged when the GPU detects that an uncorrectable error occurs on the GPU. This is also reported back to the user application. A GPU reset or node reboot is needed to clear this error. The tool nvidia-smi can provide a summary of ECC errors. If the ECC error is reported for SRAM (excludes “framebuffer”), check for SRAM DBE thresholds and follow RMA flow if exceeded - (nvidia-smi <sram_threshold_exceeded> or NSM Msg Type 0x3, Cmd Code 0x7D, bit 0). If flag is set, run field diag.

Oct 12 10:34:56 hostname kernel: NVRM: GPU 0000:02:00.0: Memory remapping activity occurred.
Oct 12 10:34:56 hostname kernel: NVRM: GPU 0000:02:00.0: Previous ECC error count: 2
Oct 12 10:34:56 hostname kernel: NVRM: GPU 0000:02:00.0: Check documentation for row remapper details.
Xid 63 logged on H100 indicates a memory remapping event due to ECC memory errors. No action is required at this time.

meta.trigger_conditions: These events are logged when the GPU handles ECC memory errors on the GPU. On GPUs that support row remapping, starting with NVIDIA® Ampere archtecture GPUs, these events provide details on row remapper activity. For more information row remapper Xids, refer to https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/a100-gpu-mem-error-mgmt/index.html#row-remapping . On earlier GPUs that support dynamic page retirement, these events provide details on dynamic page retirement activity. For more information on dynamic page retirement Xids, refer to https://docs.nvidia.com/deploy/dynamic-page-retirement/index.html .
